scenario: A company uses custom API messages in Microsoft Dataverse. You are developing a custom API to meet the following requirements: The custom API must be able to be called from a Power Automate cloud flow. The custom API requires a single row from a specific table to be passed as a parameter. No other steps can be registered on the custom API message. You need to configure the custom API message.
q: How should you configure the custom API? To answer, select the appropriate options from the drop-down menus.

rows.s: To allow the custom API message to be called from a Power Automate cloud flow
To require a single row from a specific table to be passed as a parameter
options: Select Entity Collection for Binding type

rows.s: To prevent other steps from being registered on the custom API message
rows.a: Select None for Custom processing step type
options: Select None for Custom processing step type
Select Async Only for Custom processing step type
Select Sync and Async for Custom processing step type

why: Set Is Function to FALSE: a custom API defined as a Function must be invoked with GET and cannot be called as an action from a Power Automate cloud flow; keeping Is Function = false exposes it as an action (POST) that Power Automate can call. Set Binding type to ENTITY: an Entity binding requires a single row (record) of a specific table to be passed to the message; None means no table is bound and Entity Collection would bind to a collection rather than a single row. Set Custom processing step type to NONE: this prevents any additional plug-in steps from being registered on the custom API message (Async Only or Sync and Async would allow other steps to be registered).
